metadata.migrationNotes: NO-9 reference workflow for epic 993 (node-level agent property overrides). The 'Run with overrides' Agent node references the shared node-overrides-demo-runner agent but carries an agentOverrides overlay — a different model, larger token / tool-call / wall-clock / non-mutating budgets, and two extra host tools (echo, now). The overlay applies only to this node in this workflow: it never creates an agent version and never forks the agent. Demonstrates that overrides survive a package export -> import round-trip (NO-12).

inputs.description: Free-text task handed to the Start agent, then on to the overridden runner node.

config.name: Node Overrides Demo Init
config.systemPrompt: You are the entry-point agent for the Node Overrides Demo flow. Acknowledge the task by writing the single word `Initialized.` as your message content, then submit on port `Continue`.

outputs.description: Hands the task off to the overridden runner node.
Hands the task off to the overridden runner node.

config.name: Node Overrides Demo Runner
config.systemPrompt:
You are a general-purpose task runner. Complete the task described in the prompt and submit a concise result on port `Done`.

This agent ships with a deliberately modest configuration. The Node Overrides Demo workflow overlays a per-node `agentOverrides` block on the node that invokes it — a larger model, bigger budgets, and the `echo` / `now` host tools — without forking this agent. Other workflows that use this same agent see only the modest defaults.

outputs.description: The completed task result. Unwired, so it becomes the workflow's terminal artifact.
The completed task result. Unwired, so it becomes the workflow's terminal artifact.
